channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Understanding Preeclampsia: Risks, Symptoms, and Care During Pregnancy
item.description: Preeclampsia na serious condition wey fit affect many organs during pregnancy, including kidneys, liver, brain, eyes, lungs, and even di baby for womb. E dey usually show after 20 weeks of pregnancy w...

item.title: Challenges facing the Bondi antisemitism royal commission
item.description: The Bondi royal commission into antisemitism faces significant hurdles, largely stemming from its origins and political context. Initiated under pressure rather than genuine governmental intent, the c...

item.title: Flight Operations Temporarily Suspended at Four Washington DC-Area Airports Due to Chemical Odour
item.description: On the evening of March 13, 2026, four major airports in the Washington D.C. area, including Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport, Washington Dulles International Airport, Baltimore-Washington In...

item.title: Geita Gold Mining launches network to support women in mining
item.description: Geita Gold Mining Limited (GGML), the largest gold mine in Tanzania and part of AngloGold Ashanti, recently launched the African Business Unit Women Network and Allies (AWNA). The initiative aims to s...

item.title: PTI demands immediate medical treatment for Imran Khan, threatens nationwide action
item.description: Islamabad – Leaders of Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) have voiced serious concerns about the deteriorating health of their jailed founder and former Prime Minister, Imran Khan. At a press conference i...

item.title: APC Lawmaker Clement Jimbo Denies Any Plans to Join ADC
item.description: Clement Jimbo, wey dey represent Abak/Etim Ekpo/Ika Federal Constituency for Akwa Ibom State for House of Representatives, don come out sharply reject claim say him wan leave APC join African Democrat...

item.title: Engineered Causes of Kenya’s Public Debt Accumulation
item.description: Kenya’s public debt has reached alarming levels, not due to mere misfortune but through deliberate policy choices and mismanagement. The article argues that successive governments engineered this cris...

item.title: UP Fighting Maroons’ Casiey Dongallo sidelined for rest of UAAP Season 88 due to knee injury
item.description: The UP Fighting Maroons women’s volleyball team faces a major setback as Casiey Dongallo, one of their key players, will miss the rest of UAAP Season 88 due to anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) and men...

item.title: Health Canada Reports Four Fatalities Linked to Plasma Donation in Canada Over Ten Years
item.description: Over the past decade, four individuals in Canada have died after donating plasma, with three of these fatalities occurring in Manitoba. Health Canada confirmed these cases following a CBC News investi...

item.title: Tanzania Tour Operators Retract Flood Alert for Serengeti and Apologise to Public
item.description: The Tanzania Association of Tour Operators (Tato) has officially withdrawn a public notice it issued earlier about flooding in Serengeti National Park and issued a formal apology. The notice, which wa...

item.title: Six Suspects Arraigned in Court for 2015 Murder of MP George Muchai and Aides
item.description: Six individuals have been charged in court over the brutal murder of former Kabete Member of Parliament George Muchai, his driver, and two bodyguards back in February 2015. The incident happened along...

item.title: Canadians Call for Permanent Standard Time Across All Provinces and Territories
item.description: The Globe and Mail’s letters to the editor for March 14 cover a variety of topics reflecting public opinion on social and cultural issues in Canada. A key letter advocates for making the upcoming fall...

item.title: Eastern Cape ANC councillor under investigation for alleged rent-free chalet stay
item.description: An Eastern Cape ANC ward councillor, Thulani Tempi, is facing scrutiny for reportedly living rent-free for three years in a beachfront chalet at the Gonubie Caravan Park Resort, which is owned by the ...

item.title: ATC extends interim pre-arrest bail of PTI leaders and relatives in multiple cases
item.description: LAHORE: On Friday, the Anti-Terrorism Court (ATC) extended the interim pre-arrest bail of several Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) leaders and relatives of former chairman Imran Khan, in cases linked to...

item.title: A mother’s quiet strength and the lessons of love and loyalty
item.description: The article is a heartfelt reflection on a mother’s influence and the unspoken ways she supported her child throughout life. Rather than offering overt advice or moral lessons, the author’s mother pro...

item.title: Danny Green Reflects on His Career and Impact Beyond Championships
item.description: In an interview featured by ABS-CBN Sports, veteran NBA player Danny Green shared insights about how he views his basketball career and legacy. The three-time NBA champion, who has played key roles fo...

item.title: Understanding the impact of the menstrual cycle on women’s athletic performance
item.description: As the Women’s Asian Cup continues in Australia, attention often focuses on athletes’ physical abilities like speed, strength and endurance. However, an emerging area of sports science highlights how ...

item.title: India Faces LPG Shortage Amid West Asia Conflict, Authorities Take Measures
item.description: The ongoing conflict between Iran and Israel has triggered significant disruptions in global energy supplies, affecting India’s LPG and crude oil imports. The Indian-flagged LPG carrier, Shivalik, car...

item.title: East Africa Investment Forum 2026 to support youth and fintech growth
item.description: The 2026 East Africa Investment Forum (EAIF 2026) is set to be a big stage for young innovators and technology entrepreneurs, happening in Dar es Salaam on March 26–27, 2026. The event will gather inv...

item.title: Canada Targets Gold Medals in Final Days of Winter Paralympics
item.description: Canada heads into the final weekend of the 2026 Winter Paralympics in northern Italy with strong medal prospects across multiple sports. Skier Natalie Wilkie of Salmon Arm, B.C., claimed her second go...
